subHeadline: The comparisons Land Rover buyers actually want — generation vs generation, engine vs engine, model vs model. Honest, data-backed, and built for real-world decision-making.

comparisons.title: Discovery 4 vs Discovery 5
comparisons.preview: Which generation to buy — the verdict on reliability, running costs, and long-term value. The Discovery 5 benefits from revised Ingenium engines; the Discovery 4 needs proven oil-interval history.

comparisons.title: L405 vs L460 Range Rover
comparisons.preview: Is the extra outlay worth it? We compare ownership costs, reliability, and resale value. The L405 offers the widest value-to-cost margin; the L460 is safer but at a higher entry price.

comparisons.title: TDV6 vs SDV6
comparisons.preview: What's the real difference? Performance, reliability, and replacement costs compared side-by-side. Both share the 306DT crankshaft bearing risk — the difference is power delivery and host vehicle.

comparisons.title: Ingenium vs TDV6
comparisons.preview: Which should you buy? We compare fuel economy, reliability, and long-term ownership costs. The post-2019 Ingenium is the safer bet; the TDV6 delivers more power but carries real bearing risk.

comparisons.title: Defender vs Discovery
comparisons.preview: The practical ownership comparison — space, capability, running costs, and everyday usability. The Defender holds value better; the Discovery offers more seats and family practicality.

comparisons.title: Range Rover Sport vs Discovery
comparisons.preview: Ownership costs compared — which SUV offers better value for money over 3-5 years? Both share engines and platforms; choose based on refinement versus family practicality.

bottomCta.text: Not sure which is right for you? Use our diagnostic tool to get a personalised verdict in minutes.
